choice: Return the same neutral response in all cases ('If that address has an account, we've sent a link') and only actually send mail when an account/stub exists — never reveal whether an account exists. Rate-limit link requests per email and per IP.
rationale: Because there's no public signup, the mere existence of an account is itself sensitive; a differential response (or a visible error) lets an outsider enumerate who's a user. Uniform responses + rate limiting are the standard non-enumeration auth edge. This rides on the magic-link-only (216d78f9) and login-token (93d9c012) decisions without changing them.
